title: quercus acutissima subsp. kingii
intro: Quercus acutissima subsp. kingii, also known as King's sawtooth oak, is a subspecies of evergreen oak native to Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins. The health benefits of Quercus acutissima subsp. kingii include its potential to reduce fever, relieve pain, and support immune function, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of this plant are often used to make infusions, decoctions, or teas, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
